chapter_context.summary: Christ is our advocate before the Father. John gives the new commandment to love one another and warns against loving the world and following the antichrist.

outline.text: Christ our Advocate and the Test of Knowing Him
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Christ our Advocate and the Test of Knowing Him (2:1-6), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of 1 John.

outline.text: The New Commandment and Warning Against the World
outline.explanation: This section focuses on The New Commandment and Warning Against the World (2:7-17), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of 1 John.

outline.text: Warning Against Antichrists and Abiding in Him
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Warning Against Antichrists and Abiding in Him (2:18-29), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of 1 John.

1.commentary:
### Tyndale Study Notes
**Chapter Overview: 1 John 2**
This verse contributes to the central message of 1 John, emphasizing the grace and truth revealed in Jesus Christ.
1.full_commentary:
### Matthew Henry Commentary
Matthew Henry offers devotional reflections on the spiritual impact of this passage for the believer.

### Jamieson-Fausset-Brown
JFB analysis explores the historical context and original language nuances of this portion of the New Testament.

devotional.question: How does this passage impact your daily walk with God?

cultural_historical.title: New Testament Context
cultural_historical.text: Written in the 1st century AD to diverse groups of believers across the Roman Empire.
